description: Native fns currently have no frame at all — captures and locals live in Cranelift virtual registers/stack slots that the GC tracer doesn't see. This is benign today because native fns are leaf-bodied (no allocations escape across calls) but breaks the moment native bodies acquire allocating Prims that survive a Call (e.g. ListCons of a result captured across a sub-call). Wire Cranelift's stack-map emission at safepoints and teach the GC tracer to read stack maps for native-frame fns.
title: VR.6 follow-up: Cranelift stack-map emission + GC integration
